title: Unit 3: Sorting
description: Organize array elements in ascending order using basic comparison-based algorithms.

topics.title: 3.1 Bubble Sort
topics.description: Repeatedly step through the list, compare adjacent elements and swap them if they are in the wrong order. Large values 'bubble' to the end.

ispo.desc: Receives array reference 'arr' which is mutated in-place.
Declares and stores length n of array.
Declares temporary swap variable 'temp' to hold element value.
Runs double loop structure. Compares adjacent entries and swaps them if out of order.
Modifications reflect directly on the input array object, so no value is returned.

quiz.q: What is the average and worst-case time complexity of Bubble Sort?

topics.title: 3.2 Selection Sort
topics.description: Repeatedly find the minimum element from the unsorted part and put it at the beginning. Minimizes swaps.

ispo.desc: Takes array 'arr' to perform selection sort.
Declares minIndex to track the index of smallest element found in unsorted portion.
Declares temp variable used to swap elements in memory.
Finds the smallest element in range [i...n-1] and swaps it with element at position i.

quiz.q: How many swaps does Selection Sort perform in the worst case?

topics.title: 3.3 Insertion Sort
topics.description: Virtually split the array into sorted and unsorted parts. Pick values from the unsorted part and insert them into their correct position in the sorted part.

ispo.desc: Allocates key variable to hold the element to insert, and index pointer j to traverse backwards.
Iterates from index 1. Shifts sorted elements right while they are greater than key, and inserts key into the open slot.

quiz.q: What is the best-case time complexity of Insertion Sort (occurs when the array is already sorted)?
